title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list (provided as an array of node values), reverse the list and return the values of the reversed list. The reversal should flip the direction of every `next` pointer so the original tail becomes the new head.
hints: Three pointers: prev, curr, next — classic in-place reverse.
Loop: save next, flip curr.next to prev, advance prev/curr.
Edge case: empty list — return null directly.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Determine whether a singly linked list contains a cycle. The list is encoded as `[values, pos]`, where `pos` is the 0-based index of the node that the tail's `next` points back to, or `-1` if there is no cycle. Return `true` if the list has a cycle, otherwise return `false`.
constraints: pos is -1 or a valid index
hints: Floyd's tortoise + hare: slow moves 1, fast moves 2.
Agar fast slow se mil gaya → cycle. Agar fast null hua → no cycle.
Edge: empty or single node with pos=-1 → false.

title: Middle of the Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, return the value of the middle node. If the list has an even number of nodes and therefore two middle nodes, return the value of the second middle node.
hints: Slow + fast pointer trick — slow ek step, fast do step.
Jab fast null/last hit kare, slow middle pe hoga.
Even length me slow second middle pe hi rukta hai if you advance slow whenever fast & fast.next exist.

title: Remove Nth Node From End of List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and an integer `n`, remove the `n`-th node counting from the end of the list (1-indexed) and return the values of the resulting list. It is guaranteed that `1 <= n <= length`.
hints: Dummy node use kar — head removal handle ho jayegi.
Two pointer: fast ko n+1 ahead bhej, fir dono ko saath chala.
Jab fast null hit kare, slow.next is the node to skip.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: You are given the heads of two singly linked lists `a` and `b`, each sorted in ascending order. Merge the two lists into a single list that is also sorted in ascending order and return its values. The merge should splice the existing nodes together rather than create new values.
hints: Dummy node + tail pointer build pattern.
Two pointers a, b — pick smaller, advance.
Loop end ke baad jo bacha use append kar de.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list sorted in ascending order, remove all duplicate values so that each value appears only once. Return the values of the resulting list, which must remain sorted.
hints: Single pointer walk — agar curr.val == curr.next.val toh skip.
Else move forward.
Empty / single list → return as-is.

title: Palindrome Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list (provided as an array of node values), return `true` if the sequence of values reads the same forward and backward, and `false` otherwise. An empty or single-node list is considered a palindrome.
hints: Find middle, reverse second half, compare with first half.
Or simpler: dump values into array and two-pointer compare.
Empty / single → trivially palindrome.

title: Reverse Linked List II
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and two 1-indexed positions `left` and `right` where `left <= right`, reverse the nodes from position `left` to position `right` (inclusive) in place. Return the values of the resulting list.
hints: Dummy node se start kar — head reverse ho sakta hai.
Walk to node before `left`, then in-place pointer reverse for (right - left) iterations.
Splice carefully: pre-left.next ko naya head, original-left.next ko remainder.

title: Rotate List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and an integer `k`, rotate the list to the right by `k` positions and return the resulting values. `k` may be larger than the length of the list, in which case the effective rotation is `k` modulo the length.
hints: k % length kar — bada k handle ho jata hai.
List ko circle banao (tail.next = head), fir new tail dhoondh.
New head = node after new tail; break the cycle.

title: Odd Even Linked List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, group all nodes at odd positions (1-indexed) together, followed by all nodes at even positions, and return the resulting values. The relative order within each group must be preserved. Solve it in O(1) extra space by rearranging pointers, not values.
hints: Maintain two chains: odd tail and even tail.
Iterate: odd.next = odd.next.next, even.next = even.next.next.
Finally connect odd-tail to even-head.

title: Add Two Numbers
description: You are given two non-negative integers represented as singly linked lists `a` and `b`, where each node holds a single digit and the least significant digit comes first at the head (so the number 342 is stored as 2 → 4 → 3). Add the two numbers and return the sum as a linked list in the same digit-reversed format.
constraints: No leading zeros except the number 0 itself
hints: Iterate dono lists, ek digit-by-digit add carry ke saath.
Carry next iteration me forward kar, end me agar carry > 0 toh extra node.
Different lengths handle: jo khatam hua usko 0 treat kar.

title: Swap Nodes in Pairs
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list, swap every two adjacent nodes and return the values of the resulting list. You must swap the nodes themselves by rearranging pointers, not by modifying the values. If the list has an odd number of nodes, the final lone node remains in place.
hints: Dummy node se start kar.
Maintain prev — har step me ek pair fetch karke pointers shuffle kar.
Recursion bhi clean hai but iterative O(1) space.

title: Partition List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and an integer `x`, partition the list so that all nodes with value strictly less than `x` come before all nodes with value greater than or equal to `x`. The original relative order of the nodes within each of the two partitions must be preserved. Return the values of the resulting list.
hints: Build do separate lists: less and greaterEqual, dummy heads ke saath.
Iterate original, append nodes accordingly.
Last me less.tail.next = greaterEqual.head, greaterEqual.tail.next = null.

title: Reorder List
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list `L0 → L1 → ... → Ln-1`, reorder it in place to `L0 → Ln-1 → L1 → Ln-2 → L2 → ...`, interleaving nodes from the front and back. You may not modify the node values, only rearrange the nodes. Return the values of the resulting list.
hints: Steps: find middle, reverse second half, then merge two halves alternately.
After reverse, first half ends right before middle.
Be careful: split list into two halves cleanly (first half tail.next = null).

title: Linked List Cycle II
description: Given a singly linked list that may contain a cycle, return the value of the node where the cycle begins, or `null` if there is no cycle. The list is encoded as an array `arr` of node values and an integer `pos`, the 0-based index of the node that the tail's `next` points back to (or `-1` for no cycle).
hints: Phase 1: tortoise+hare to find a meeting point inside the cycle.
Phase 2: reset slow to head; advance both 1 step — they meet at entry.
Math: distance from head to entry = distance from meeting to entry (mod cycle).

title: Merge k Sorted Lists
description: You are given an array `lists` of `k` singly linked lists, each sorted in ascending order and encoded as an array of integers. Merge all of them into a single list sorted in ascending order and return its values.
hints: Pairwise merge: O(N log k).
Or: min-heap of k heads, pop smallest, push its next.
Single dummy + tail builder for the result.

title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
description: Given the `head` of a singly linked list and an integer `k`, reverse the nodes of the list `k` at a time and return the values of the resulting list. Nodes within each consecutive group of `k` are reversed in place; if the number of remaining nodes at the end is fewer than `k`, leave them in their original order.
hints: Walk in chunks of k. For each chunk, peek ahead to see if k nodes remain.
If yes, reverse those k nodes and re-link with prev tail.
If no, stop. Track group's previous tail to splice.

title: Sort List
description: Given the `head` of an unsorted singly linked list, sort it in ascending order and return the resulting values. Aim for O(n log n) time and O(1) extra space, excluding the bookkeeping inherent to the chosen algorithm.
hints: Merge sort fits linked lists naturally — split, sort halves, merge.
Iterative bottom-up version achieves O(1) extra space (no recursion stack).
Helper: split head into two halves using slow/fast.
